content: Vd determines loading dose calculation: Loading dose = Target concentration × Vd / Bioavailability. For drugs with large Vd, substantial loading doses are needed to achieve therapeutic levels quickly (e.g., digoxin, amiodarone). In obesity: lipophilic drugs (benzodiazepines, propofol) distribute into adipose tissue requiring weight-based dosing using total body weight; hydrophilic drugs (aminoglycosides) use adjusted body weight. In elderly: decreased total body water reduces Vd for hydrophilic drugs, increasing plasma concentrations. In ascites/edema: increased extracellular fluid increases Vd for hydrophilic drugs, potentially requiring higher doses. Vd changes in pregnancy due to increased plasma volume and body fat.
